items.briefing:
Phishing attacks are growing more sophisticated thanks to AI tools that help criminals craft convincing fake emails at scale — a serious concern for all staff handling sensitive information or student data. Separately, with the FIFA World Cup 2026 underway, cybercriminals are targeting fans with scam ticketing sites, fake streaming links, and fraudulent competitions. A recent ESET report also found 45% of small and medium businesses experienced a cyber incident in the past year, a reminder that no organisation is too small to be targeted.

**Practical tip:** Be extra cautious with any World Cup-related emails or links — only use official, verified websites for tickets or streaming.

featured.title: ESET report finds 45% of SMBs hit by cyber incidents
featured.quote: Nearly half of Australian SMBs suffered cyber incidents despite believing they were protected.
